title: DAVID — Black Hole Shadow Science Proof (M87*, EHT 2019)
concept: 480p marquee science proof — narration + honesty labels + NASA/EHT sources card

config.voice_suffix: measured clear science narration voice, mid-low resonant precise diction, wonder without hype, documentary trust, accessible never stuffy, synthetic host only

voice_recommendation.primary: clear enthusiastic science communicator voice, precise but accessible diction, wonder without hype, synthetic presenter only
voice_recommendation.david_lane: measured clear science narration voice, mid-low resonant precise diction, wonder without hype, documentary trust, accessible never stuffy, synthetic host only
voice_recommendation.rationale: Primary rec matches Julian science-explainer lane (cool-clinical, wonder without hype). DAVID lane keeps Archive gravitas while adopting science register.

science_subject.key_measurement: Event Horizon Telescope — first horizon-scale image, 10 April 2019

sources.citation: Event Horizon Telescope Collaboration, ApJL 875:L1 (2019) — First M87* Event Horizon Telescope Results

shots.speech_text: What does a black hole actually look like? Not a hole you could fall through — a shadow, smaller than the event horizon itself.
shots.video_prompt: CONTINUITY LOCK @David-001: identical host face, charcoal sweater, Archive desk, brass lamp, same eye-line. The Archive host speaks directly to camera with measured clear science narration voice, lip-synced, delivering: "What does a black hole actually look like? Not a hole you could fall through — a shadow, smaller than the event horizon itself." Wonder without hype, precise accessible diction, documentary trust. Medium shot, warm brass lamp 3200K. synthetic host only.

shots.speech_text: In 2019, the Event Horizon Telescope linked radio dishes worldwide and photographed the shadow of M87's supermassive black hole.
shots.video_prompt: CONTINUITY LOCK @David-001: identical host face, charcoal sweater, Archive desk, brass lamp. Host explains evidence-forward, lip-synced: "In 2019, the Event Horizon Telescope linked radio dishes worldwide and photographed the shadow of M87's supermassive black hole." Measured clear science narration voice, gesture toward evidence, medium shot. synthetic host only.

shots.speech_text: An illustrative render: a dark central shadow, a thin glowing accretion disk, and starlight bent around it — the geometry Einstein predicted.
shots.video_prompt: CONTINUITY LOCK @David-001: identical host face, charcoal sweater, Archive desk. Full-frame photorealistic scientific visualization on a large display behind the host: supermassive black hole with dark central shadow (no solid surface), thin relativistic accretion disk temperature gradient inner blue-white outer orange-red, Doppler beaming on inclined disk, gravitational lensing of background starfield, subtle Einstein ring, no luminous sphere, no waterfall accretion. Host narrates to camera lip-synced: "An illustrative render: a dark central shadow, a thin glowing accretion disk, and starlight bent around it — the geometry Einstein predicted." Measured clear science narration voice. Medium shot, viz dominates frame. synthetic host only.
